Fake methodology

How IrtyshNet doesn't work.

Step 1 — Signal extraction. 37 invented urban-vibe signals are sampled from a photo and a name, none of which correspond to any real measurement.
Step 2 — Steppe regression. A proprietary model with no training data whatsoever assigns a resilience coefficient.
Step 3 — Aura composite. Results are blended with a random number generator and presented with full confidence.
This is satire. PavloDAR AI does not detect, infer, or claim any real ethnicity, nationality, or geographic origin from any input.
PAVLODAR
01
Upload a face
Drop a photo. Any face works — we only pretend to look at it.
02
Enter a name
Full name preferred. Nicknames reduce aura precision by up to 40%.
03
Receive your score
IrtyshNet returns your Pavlodar Aura Score in under three seconds.
Live demo

Run your own aura scan.

Same name, same photo, same score — every time. IrtyshNet is nothing if not consistent about its nonsense.

Photo Optional — skipping it reduces nothing, since nothing is measured.

Aura scores are fictional. No cultural, ethnic, or geographic claims implied.

Awaiting subject. The steppe is patient.

    FAQ

    Questions, answered plainly.

    Is this scientifically valid?
    Emotionally, yes. Statistically, no.
    Do you store photos?
    No, we only store the vibe. Photos are discarded immediately after scanning.
    Can this identify where someone is from?
    No. It only measures fictional aura compatibility — it cannot and does not infer real ethnicity, nationality, or origin.
